message:
perf(distributed): refactor distribute() to bounded concurrent sends

Replace the sequential `for assignment in assignments` loop in
SymbolDistributor::distribute() with a bounded-parallelism approach
using SelectAllDrain, implementing the long-standing TODO for
concurrent execution that respects max_concurrent.

Key changes:

- Sends are launched as boxed futures (SendFuture) up to
  config.max_concurrent at a time. As each completes, the next
  assignment from the iterator is enqueued, maintaining full
  utilisation of the concurrency budget.

- Introduce a symbols_sent_total counter that tracks actual symbols
  dispatched, skipping empty assignments. The previous approach used
  encoded.symbols.len() which overcounts when some replicas receive
  no symbols.

- Extract evaluate_outcomes_with_sent() to accept an explicit sent
  count; the original evaluate_outcomes() is kept as a thin wrapper
  for backwards compatibility. Metrics accumulation now uses
  saturating_add instead of casting through u32 which could silently
  truncate on large symbol sets.

- Add MockSuccessTransport and a test
  (distribute_counts_symbols_sent_per_replica_attempt) that verifies
  the symbol-count metric matches the sum across all replica
  assignments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
